title: verbascum calycinum
intro: Verbascum calycinum, also known as the mullein flower or woolly mullein, is a biennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high saponin content. The health benefits of Verbascum calycinum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Verbascum calycinum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as salves and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
